node.statement: QED: The error-correction transition at [f_2]_crit = (1-r)(1+g_{s,e}). Since f_2 grows linearly with depth t (errors accumulate), maintaining fixed f_2 requires γ ~ 1/t. This means the critical noise rate decreases as 1/t, and achieving perfect recovery requires t = ω(N). For weak unital noise: F ≈ e^{-γNt}, so γ ≤ log d(1-r)/(2t). Finite-depth corrections scale as O(1/t), confirmed by Fig.2(d). [Eq.(21), main.tex line 282]
